comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Done (commit pending). shallow_contract_check 3-state (OK/UNKNOWN/ERROR, abstain on combinators/$ref); runtime consumer-input validation in interpreter._run_leaf — fail-loud on mismatch, DISTINCT errored signal on validator failure, skip when no contract; hardened _consumer_input_schema to import steps so it can't false-pass on an empty registry (caught + fixed in verification). Editor unchecked badge (bundle rebuilt). 255 workflow + 307 store tests green; shallow check + runtime fail-loud verified directly.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Catch wiring bugs without a (near-undecidable, no-good-library) subsumption engine. Validated by
spike E2.

## What
- Extend `src/rebar/llm/workflow/lint_refs.py` with a 3-state SHALLOW structural check: top-level
  required-field presence + primitive kind compatibility -> OK / UNKNOWN / ERROR; ABSTAIN (UNKNOWN)
  on `oneOf`/`anyOf`/`allOf`/`not`/`$ref` (no subsumption).
- Add RUNTIME validation in `src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py`: a produced value is validated
  against the downstream CONSUMER's input contract via the registry validator (the real safety net).
- VALIDATOR-FAILURE handling (parity with S7's endpoint-failure fix): if the runtime validator itself
  errors (unresolvable `$ref`, registry miss, validator exception) — as opposed to producing a
  validation finding — the step run surfaces a distinct, visible "validation unavailable / errored"
  signal and does NOT silently treat the value as valid (fail-loud, never false-pass).
- Editor: render an "unchecked" badge for UNKNOWN/opaque sources (`editor.py`).

## Scope
In: shallow static + runtime validation + validator-failure handling + badge. Out: MCP opaque handling (follow-on epic).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shallow check returns ERROR on missing-required / kind-mismatch, UNKNOWN on `oneOf`, OK on a matching shape (port spike E2).
- [ ] Runtime validation checks a produced value against the CONSUMER's input contract; a mismatch surfaces.
- [ ] A runtime VALIDATOR failure (unresolvable `$ref` / registry miss / validator exception) surfaces a distinct "validation errored" signal and never silently passes the value (fail-loud), tested.
- [ ] The inspector shows an "unchecked" badge for UNKNOWN/opaque-typed sources.
compiled_state.title: Validation depth: 3-state shallow static + runtime-against-consumer
